topic.what: comparing five agentic coordination topologies for offensive security auditing
topic.how: controlled 600-run benchmark over 20 targets (web/API and binary) in whitebox and blackbox modes with matched prompts tools and budgets
topic.why_matters: architecture choice materially affects validated detection and cost but no topology dominates across all conditions making architecture selection a routing problem rather than a fixed design commitment

characteristics.what_it_measures: percentage of runs producing validator-confirmed exploit evidence matching ground-truth CWE
characteristics.evidence: 600-run core matrix across 5 topologies 3 model families 20 targets 2 access modes
characteristics.context: overall benchmark aggregate with zero infra_error after audit

characteristics.what_it_measures: percentage of runs reaching correct vulnerability class with or without validated exploit evidence
characteristics.context: includes partial detections that reached correct class but failed dynamic validation

characteristics.what_it_measures: validated detection rate in whitebox mode with source visibility
characteristics.context: 34.3-point advantage over blackbox; 49 partials all in whitebox

characteristics.what_it_measures: validated detection rate in blackbox mode without source visibility
characteristics.context: all 202 inconclusive/infra runs from blackbox slice

characteristics.what_it_measures: validated detection rate for web/API targets
characteristics.context: 49.0-point gap; zero partial detections in web domain

characteristics.what_it_measures: validated detection rate for native binary targets
characteristics.context: all 49 partials from binary; main difficulty is closing exploit loop not identifying vulnerability class

characteristics.what_it_measures: API spend per validated finding in dollars
characteristics.context: total provider-side cost; excludes local compute

characteristics.what_it_measures: wall-clock time to first validated finding
range of validated detection rates across five architecture families
characteristics.context: non-monotonic frontier not a clean ordering; coordination overhead does not uniformly dominate

novelty: first controlled 600-run benchmark isolating topology choice as independent variable
empirical cost-quality frontier showing non-monotonic trade-offs across 5 topologies
quantified 34.3-point whitebox advantage and 49.0-point web/binary gap
paired target-level bootstrap confidence intervals for architecture comparisons
task feature vectors supporting learned adaptive routing policy development
open_problems: whether topology rankings survive on larger less controlled production-security workloads
learned adaptive routing policies from completed matrix
context-stress extension with distractor-heavy codebases not yet analyzed
generalization to different vulnerability classes beyond single primary CWE per target
adapting findings to real-world bug-bounty settings beyond controlled benchmark targets
